feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: What a delusional ego
entry.content: What a delusional ego

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >say you play a hard game >"Thats just artificial difficulty/gookclick/kaizoshit…
entry.content: >say you play a hard game >"Thats just artificial difficulty/gookclick/kaizoshit/a shmup/a fighting game/memorization slop" >say you play an easy game >"Thats way too easy/yellow paint/back then we went to school up 2 hills both ways during snow/ruined the industry" Which one is it?

entry.title: /b/ Random: You go back in time. What do you do?
entry.content: You go back in time. What do you do?

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >Pathologic man, Pathologic. It's like, I'm saving it. I just know at some point…
entry.content: >Pathologic man, Pathologic. It's like, I'm saving it. I just know at some point in my life as a man who's an empath, I'm really gonna NEED it, and I want it to be there for me. That's why I could only get through 20 minutes of it, but even in that 20 minutes, WOW is there a lot to dissect there. I could talk endlessly about it but I won't bore you with platitudes hahaha. So anyway, can I see your pussy?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: With most of the traffic online coming less and less from actual people, compani…
entry.content: With most of the traffic online coming less and less from actual people, companies are going to shit the bed if they rely on traditional ad revenue and paywalled content. With the revival of x402, companies will start switching to API dumps that just exist to feed info to LLMs and profit through the 402 transaction. It's not hard to see that the future of the internet will largely be humans interacting with chatbots for their latest content instead of directly visiting websites themselves.

entry.title: /int/ International: This is the coolest region of Turkey. It has the same climate with Southern Cana…
entry.content: This is the coolest region of Turkey. It has the same climate with Southern Canada which is Dfb(Warm-summer humid continental) and Dfc(Subarctic) in the highlands. Thoughts?

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Have I dodged a bullet
entry.content: I stopped watching at the cliffhanger of season 3 where soldier boy explodes. I've seen clips here and there and wanted to continue because Oh Father seemed like a cool character and then this fucking scene, seriously? Were they on a budget or just wanted to wrap it up asap while bringing down muh toxic masculinity? I don't get it. What is the point of ruining something that you've created. Do jews have a fetish for ruining shit that seemed promising? I mean that's a rhetorical question. Also wh

entry.title: /news/ News: DHS Wants to Create Its Own Round-The-Clock Deportation Airline
entry.content: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/arti cles/2026-07-10/dhs-wants-to-create -its-own-round-the-clock-deportatio n-airline The US government is moving ahead with plans to create its own fleet of government-owned aircraft to bolster deportations. The Department of Homeland Security is beginning a search for a company to operate planes that would help carry out deportation flights, respond to emergencies and transport senior officials. The department asked aviation companies this week to describe how t

entry.content: I'm pissed off, /b/. Here's what happened today: >be me, 30-something yo, running a tech company >Fairly well-traveled, experienced picking up girls on trips. Happily married now with a cute wife, but I stay in shape. >One of my longest-time employees, a programmer, is a perma-virgin. >Picture the stereotype of unfuckable programmer: that's him 100%. >Tried setting him up with single girl friends multiple times. Nothing ever stuck. >One was even into him, but he didn't find her cute enough (I wo

entry.content: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nTI fzXx8nJY >Wendy (Fanning) finds her life upended when her boyfriend Jack (Johnson)—older, divorced, and with children—asks for space to reassess their relationship. During their separation, Wendy reconnects with her ex, Chuck (Smith), sparking an emotionally charged love triangle that forces all three to confront the realities of love, timing, and personal growth. A WHITE LOVE TRIANGLE? WHO WILL GET THAT BLUE COLLAR FANNING PUSSY? THE OLDER SINGLE FATHER OR TH

No limits past site rules; use Catbox galleries and uploads if needed. Keep dumps to a minimum and don't be a derailing cunt, thanks. entry.title: /g/ Technology: Alright I’ll bite
entry.content: does this stuff actually work?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: home PSUs are fucked up
entry.content: its a bit impossible to hook up 8x 3.5 inch HDDs onto modern desktop computers similarly 16x 2,5" are impossible (altough its easy to fit them space wise) whats gonna happen with 16x 2.5" then, you ask? well they need 16x 1amps from 5v line, thats 80 amps guess what is the maximum on modern PSUs on 5 volt line? its 100 watts the rest, 20 watts which you still should have, certainly goes to other parts in your comptuer and its not even enough, realistically a computer would take 120 watts from 5v

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Europe: In-Car Cameras Are Now Required
entry.content: All new cars in Europe must have an advanced driver-monitoring system using cameras. https://www.thedrive.com/news/in-ca r-cameras-are-now-required-in-europ e-us-isnt-far-behind >A slate of laws impacting new cars has just gone into effect in the European Union as of Wednesday, and it’s likely to have some opponents. The bloc is now mandating that every vehicle come equipped with an interior-facing camera as part of an “advanced driver distraction warning (ADDW) system”.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Brent Hinds RIP
entry.content: Have you guys seen this? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ba4 fW0mXh_w Mastodon's farewell letter to Brent Hinds and his difficult, alcoholism fueled persona that eventually led to him getting fired from the band and probably his alcohol fueled accident that led to his death. Its a very raw, eye-openign and honest video. Ive never seen anything like it. Everyone who ever dealt with an alcoholic should watch this. Guy was the most genius guitar player of his generation.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Age Verification
entry.content: I live in Texas, and that means every goddamn thing I do online now requires age verification. Typically I'm presented with a battery of choices to verify my age, different companies and different methods. Which should I choose, and which should I avoid? Any personal experience stories would be appreciated. (inb4 VPN. That's a different conversation.)

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Making my own MMO server cause you are all worthless
entry.content: Fuck you and fuck modern video games. I'm done. To anyone that used to play Lineage 2 in the past(mostly eastern eurofags), I've been working for over a year on recreating a server that was exactly like the old L2Pride on Interlude. 2007 Version. Customized and PvP heavy, meaning that we expect a lot of players to make this viable. Will also feature a custom race war system along with rewards for castle owners and more If you are interested join the discord here: https://discord.com/invite/2Knae

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Endings That Make You Think
entry.content: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-fK bzX7-I2Y >Amanda falls unconscious, and later awakens on a gurney. Several doctors, in protective hazmat suits, inform her that she is in a hospital and they will help her. The doctors reveal that the "creatures" were escaped patients, and after realizing that Amanda "knows too much," she is then forced into a dark cell and is swarmed by the recaptured patients, as the doctor closes the door's viewing shutter.

entry.content: You buy 22 cores but can only use 8 or 6 at a time, what’s the point in that? And the rest are some ‘power-saving’ ones. Instead of having fixed cores like AMD, where you use all of them that you actually paid for

entry.content: >so....take us through my divorce. >your divorce? oh man, I mean, what can you say, it was definitely a divorce. There have been a lot of divorces in the last few months, so like any other year it would have been a divorce of the year contender, but it...it definitely was still the best divorce of the weekend. I guess the deal is, your wife, she, like, was sick, I mean, basically she was sick of your bullshit. Yeah, it's like, you know, you bought her a yoga studio, a beach house, a vanity chari

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Linus Torvalds only uses email and Git now.
entry.content: https://www.zdnet.com/article/open- source-summit-linus-torvalds/ >Torvalds no longer sees himself as a programmer. "Let's be entirely honest. I hardly read code at all anymore. I'm not a programmer, I'm a development lead." >He said he dives into code mainly when something forces his attention, like build breaks or merge conflicts: "I've done so many conflict resolutions over the years that I could probably do them in my sleep… Quite often at that point, when I look at the code, I sometimes fin

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Alien Earth season 2 will be weird, urgent and relevant
entry.content: >Here’s what I can tell you — shit’s gonna get weird. I’ve read the first six [episodes], and they’re really, really, really good. It’s just the best of Noah Hawley. Surprising, wonderful characters, rich world. I feel like it’s just matured into a really dynamic story — relevant and urgent and funny and fucked up.” https://www.avpgalaxy.net/2026/06/2 4/alien-earth-season-2-is-gonna-get -weird-says-timothy-olyphant/

Tim Walz and other top state officials. The statement said 42-year-old Tou Lue Vang was arrested by U.S.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Ads
entry.content: People hate ads on the internet, but without them we wouldn't had many of the "free" services we use daily, imagine if you needed to pay to access most websites or online services, many times you'd use them only once and never return, it's the same as those paywalled articles, no one wants to pay, even if it's only a symbolic price. I think it's a fair trade, let all the normies watch ads so services can make money, while we /g/ 1337 h4x0rs use adblockers. It's ugly, but this is one of the best

According to the source, it uses a form of Balinese healing that essentially returns to body to its factory-default virgin status. How can men cope after this? Making western women virgins again will essentially make their status sky rocket in the dating market

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Isn't piracy dying?
entry.content: I see many people saying that piracy is thriving, consumers backlashing against greedy companies, abandon subscription services and resort to piracy but in reality I see the opposite. Sites are closing one after another (especially rom, emulation and anime sites), torrents are slowly dying, storage is becoming unaffordable, burners are out of production. Piracy was huge because of cheap storage (hdds, optical burners, high quality discs) and high percentage of pc users and tech savvy enthusiasts

entry.title: /int/ International: Over 500 children in peru named Haaland
entry.content: >The Norwegian football star's success has led to a flood of children being named after him, reports the Peruvian outlet RPP. The state registry office, Reniec, stated in a press release that the Norwegian's name appears in various forms: 4 have been given the full name, Erling Braut Haaland. 91 have been given the name Erling Haaland. 468 have been given the name Haaland. In total, 563 children have been named after the Norwegian striker.

entry.title: /int/ International: >Whatever, in the showers at the gym I realized I had a really small dick. I mea…
entry.content: >Whatever, in the showers at the gym I realized I had a really small dick. I measured it when I got home—it was twelve centimeters, maybe thirteen or fourteen if you measured right to the base. I'd found something new to worry about, something I couldn't do anything about; it was a basic and permanent handicap. It was around then that I started hating blacks. >There weren't many of them in the school—most of them went to the technical high school, Lycée Pierre-de-Coubertin, where the eminent Def
